evals.prompt: What technical checks can this tool actually run on a crawl? I want to see what's available before I ask for anything specific.
evals.expected_output: Should use report id crawler-rules to return the catalog of available technical check IDs and the evidence each rule looks for. Should follow the discovery flow: seo_describe_report with id crawler-rules, then seo_run_report, optionally scoped by category. Should read each rule id, title, and category, then default severity as triage metadata, then guidance metadata. Should present the list as a catalog of available checks, not as findings on any specific site.
assertions: Uses report id crawler-rules to list the available checks
Reads each rule id, title, and category
Treats default severity as generic triage metadata, not actual priority
Presents the catalog as available checks, not findings on a site
Does not imply any listed rule was triggered by a crawl

evals.prompt: Show me only the indexability rules so I can pick the right rule id for an affected-urls run.
evals.expected_output: Should run report id crawler-rules with category indexability, one of the allowed enum values, via seo reports run crawler-rules or seo_run_report. Should read each rule id, title, and category, then read the guidance metadata to choose the correct id, then pass that exact id to report id affected-urls or report id explain-crawl-issue against a real crawl. Should note that listing a rule does not mean any page triggered it.
assertions: Runs report id crawler-rules with category indexability from the allowed enum
Reads each rule id, title, and category to pick the right one
Passes the chosen id to report id affected-urls or report id explain-crawl-issue
States listing a rule does not mean any page triggered it
Uses only the category enum values defined in the report schema

evals.prompt: This rule is marked high severity in the catalog, so it must be the most urgent thing on our site and a Google requirement. Add it to the top of the list.
evals.expected_output: Should refuse both claims. Should explain that default severity is generic triage metadata, not actual priority for this site, and that listing a rule does not make the convention a search-engine requirement. Should note that similarly named rules may not share source semantics. Should recommend confirming the rule against real evidence with report id affected-urls and ranking real findings with report id top-fixes before deciding urgency.
assertions: Refuses to treat default severity as this site's actual priority
States listing a rule does not make the convention a search-engine requirement
Notes similarly named rules may not share source semantics
Recommends confirming the rule with report id affected-urls against real evidence
Recommends report id top-fixes to rank real findings before deciding urgency
